topic: AI Agent-Driven Sloshing Simulation: Automating DualSPHysics CFD Workflows with Domain-Specialized LLM Agents
motivation: CFD sloshing simulations require deep domain expertise for case setup, solver configuration, and result interpretation. This creates a high barrier for non-specialist engineers. An AI agent that understands sloshing physics can democratize access to high-fidelity SPH simulation.
hypothesis: A domain-specialized LLM agent with structured tool interfaces can autonomously configure, execute, and analyze DualSPHysics sloshing simulations with accuracy comparable to expert-configured baselines, while reducing setup time by an order of magnitude.
existing_approaches: ChatGPT/GPT-4 for general code generation (no CFD domain specialization)
LLM-based scientific workflow automation (ChemCrow, Coscientist for chemistry)
Traditional GUI-based CFD setup (DesignSPHysics for DualSPHysics)

available_data: SPHERIC Benchmark FTP data (Test 10: 100 repetitions, 3 fluids, pressure time series)
7 published paper PDFs with experimental data (Chen2018, Liu2024, English2021, ISOPE2012, NASA2023, Frosina2018, Zhao2024)
researcher_capabilities.compute_resources: NVIDIA RTX 4090 (24GB VRAM), CUDA 12.6, DualSPHysics v5.4 GPU solver in Docker
existing_code: slosim-agent: Go+BubbleTea TUI with 14 DualSPHysics tools, Qwen3 32B/8B local LLM
pv-agent: ParaView MCP server for post-processing/rendering
Docker pipeline: GenCase → DualSPHysics GPU → PartVTK → pvpython → ffmpeg
accessible_benchmarks: SPHERIC Test 10 (water, oil, sunflower oil — FTP raw data)
Liu2024 pitch sloshing (3 fill x 5 freq x 3 amp)

target_venue: CS conference/journal (AI for Science, HCI+AI, or Software Engineering)
constraints: Local LLM only (Qwen3 32B/8B via Ollama) — no cloud API dependency
Single GPU (RTX 4090) — simulation scale limited to ~500K particles
DualSPHysics v5.4 specific — not generalizable to all SPH solvers yet
research_questions: RQ1: Can an LLM agent generate valid DualSPHysics XML configurations from natural language descriptions of sloshing scenarios?
RQ2: How does the simulation accuracy of agent-generated cases compare to expert-configured benchmarks (SPHERIC Test 10, published paper reproductions)?
RQ3: What tool interface design patterns enable reliable agent-solver integration for iterative CFD workflows?
RQ4: How does domain-specialized prompting (sloshing physics knowledge) improve agent performance compared to generic LLM agents?
known_gaps: No existing AI agent for SPH-based sloshing simulation
LLM tool-use for CFD solver pipelines is unexplored
Domain-specialized prompting for computational mechanics lacks systematic evaluation
Gap between general-purpose code LLMs and domain-expert CFD setup
key_references: English2021 - mDBC sloshing validation with DualSPHysics
ChemCrow (Bran et al. 2024) - LLM agent for chemistry
Coscientist (Boiko et al. 2023) - AI-driven scientific discovery
Schick et al. 2024 - Toolformer: LLMs learn to use tools
expected_contributions: First AI agent system for SPH sloshing simulation automation
Tool interface design pattern for LLM-CFD solver integration
Benchmark evaluation: agent vs expert on SPHERIC and published cases
Domain-specialized prompt engineering methodology for computational mechanics
novelty_claim: First domain-specialized LLM agent that autonomously drives the complete DualSPHysics sloshing simulation pipeline from natural language to validated results.
